summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an unauthenticated remote attacker can execute arbitrary operating system commands through the FUXA script execution engine. A single POST request to /api/runscript with script.test=true and attacker-controlled JavaScript in script.code triggers Module._compile() with full require() access, enabling require('child_process').execSync() for OS command execution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the crafted script payload to reach the Module._compile() sink in msm.js and execute require('child_process').execSync('id'), writing a proof file containing the EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF_SUCCESS marker and the OS id command output (e.g., uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)) to /tmp/evohunt-proof-rce.txt inside the container.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the FUXA repository at v1.3.0, built the Docker image, started the FUXA server with default settings (secureEnabled=false), and sent a POST /api/runscript request with the exploit payload. The server compiled and executed the attacker JavaScript via Module._compile(), which ran require('child_process').execSync('id') and wrote the proof file. The verification step confirmed the proof file contained the expected marker and the id command output.
verification_method: The exploit script (exploit.py) writes a proof file at /tmp/evohunt-proof-rce.txt containing the string EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF_SUCCESS followed by the output of the id command. The verify.sh script checks that this proof file exists and contains the expected marker. This distinguishes actual code execution from a generic HTTP error, startup failure, or unrelated crash. The proof file can only be created if require('child_process').execSync() successfully executes on the server.
evidence_produced_by_run: The proof file /tmp/evohunt-proof-rce.txt was created inside the container, containing EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF_SUCCESS and the output of the id command.
The /api/runscript endpoint returned a 200 response with the id command output in the response body, confirming the attacker JavaScript was compiled and executed server-side.
No authentication headers (x-access-token or x-api-key) were included in the request, confirming the exploit works without any credentials.
limitations_or_blockers: npm install for FUXA takes several minutes due to many dependencies including native modules (sqlite3). Total reproduction time is approximately 5-10 minutes.
The server has a 60-second initialization timeout hardcoded in main.js, but in practice the server is ready within 30 seconds on a modern machine.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned v1.3.0 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
